questions.question: According to the 80/20 rule of solo founder legal, what percentage of contracts can be handled with good templates?
questions.explanation: 80% of your contracts can be handled with good templates and common sense. 20% require professional review. The skill is knowing which is which.

questions.question: At what deal value threshold should you always involve legal counsel?
questions.explanation: When total contract value exceeds $50,000, a $500-800 legal review is trivially small relative to the financial exposure. Send the contract with specific questions marked — don't ask for a general review.

questions.question: What is the average cost of a business contract dispute in legal fees?
questions.explanation: Average cost of a business contract dispute is $25,000-$100,000 in legal fees — even when you win. A $300-800 flat-fee contract review for prevention makes far more financial sense.

questions.question: You should involve legal counsel whenever a client asks you to transfer (not license) IP ownership.
questions.explanation: True. A license says 'you can use this.' A transfer says 'this is now yours forever.' Once IP is transferred, you can't use it. Get a lawyer to make sure you understand exactly what you're giving up.

questions.question: Review your current and upcoming deals. Which ones meet the threshold for legal review (over $50K, IP transfer, non-compete)? How would you scope the legal review to keep costs under $800?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response identifies specific deals or scenarios that trigger the counsel requirement, understands the distinction between licensing and transferring IP, and proposes a scoped legal review approach (specific questions marked rather than general review) to control costs.
